ab: acʉhʉ ʉmnʉ, maitan’e te hafa ta lema’cohio ho uhto you ho tuhtuucosʉ to mamameoi.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
en: A: Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.

ab: na’no ʉmnʉ, teto uhto mamameoi ho bua cou.
ch: B:好喔!我們要跟耆老說鄒語。
en: B: Okay! We’re going to speak Chowder with the seniors.
A: Are we almost there?
B:Almost there.

ab: ak’i, mimza uhtan’e ho aiti suu.
en: A: Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.

ab: ’oaa! ma mimu meelʉ bua cou! a’ʉmtʉ na’no ʉmnʉ!
ch: B:哇! 你們會說鄒語啊!真是太棒了!
en: B: Wow! You speak the Tsou language! That’s great!

ab: acʉhʉ ʉmnʉ, maitan’e te hafa ta lema’cohio ho uhto toton’u ho yaahioa.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
en: A:Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.
B: Yes, sir.

ab: acʉhʉ uhtan’e ho bitoyange, talʉa hafa ’o te tititha.
ch: A:大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
en: A: Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.

ab: zou, lema’cohio, imza cu acʉha hafa ’ete tititha.
ch: B:是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
en: B: Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.
A: Are we there yet?
B: Not yet, it’s still far away.
A: You go watering.
B: Okay, I’ll water it.
A: You go get the bird catcher.
B: Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

ab: acʉhʉ ʉmnʉ, teto uhto emoo no mayuyuansou maitan’e.
ch: A:大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
en: A: Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.

ab: na’no ʉmnʉ, teto mainenu hote uhne’e?
ch: B:好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
en: B: Awesome! How are we going to get there?
A: Let’s take the bus.

ab: acʉhʉ bitoyange, teto ayocʉ mhia kipu.
ch: A:大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
en: A: Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.
B: Yes.

ab: zou mazozomʉ sica, zou mafkofkoi ta’e. acʉhʉ iachi bobaito.
ch: A:前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
en: A: There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.
B: I’m afraid of snakes.
A: It doesn’t matter, then you go and watch the birds.

ch: A:我們一個小時後回來集合。
en: A: We’ll be back in an hour.
B: Yes, thank you.

ab: zou cuma na iko aiti maitan’e?
en: A: What did you see today?

ab: mo man’i ’o os’o aiti ci fkoi. ho ma’i ’o zomʉ.
ch: B:我看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
en: B: I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.
A: Hurry up, we have to go.

ab: totea na’a a’o, mo ngoseo co t’ango’u.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.
A: Shall we go swimming together?
B:Yes! Let’s go!
A: There are a lot of people swimming here.

ab: mo na’no fozu ta chumu ta’e, tetoc’o yontan’e ho yuuhnguzu.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
